summary: Simon Späti shares his experience transitioning from traditional corporate work to a location-independent lifestyle, documenting his journey from Copenhagen to Bali, Southeast Asia, and across Europe. He argues that working remotely while traveling enables deep focus and natural productivity breaks, creating an ideal work-life integration. The post outlines the mindset and skills needed for this lifestyle, emphasizing creativity, continuous learning, and starting small. He reflects on lessons learned including the importance of cross-cultural connections, stoic philosophy, and maintaining a home base while embracing mobility.
key_points: Remote work while traveling enables deep focus sessions of 2-3 hours with natural breaks during sightseeing that help when stuck on problems
The location-independent lifestyle requires specific values: creativity, loving your work, starting small, celebrating wins, and continuous reading
Stepping out of comfort zones by moving to unfamiliar environments forces rapid personal and professional growth
Having a home base is compatible with the nomadic lifestyle - you don't need to travel constantly
Cross-cultural exposure reveals our fundamental human similarities despite different backgrounds
money_quotes: While having a delicious coffee, I can get things done for 2-3 hours.
Value the time, spend it right, you can't buy it back!
Maybe most important, getting to know so many different cultures and realising that we are all the same, just with a different background.
You don't have to travel all the time. It's okay to have a base somewhere in the world, where you can enjoy old friends, family and loved once and prepare for the next trip.
That was the first step for me to get out of my comfort zone and force myself to learn a bunch of new things.
key_insight: The location-independent lifestyle isn't about constant travel but about intentionally designing work around deep focus sessions while remaining open to growth through new environments and cultures.
